content:
hello, I'm looking for a good place to contribute to Rust-for-Linux. I've been following the issue tracker on Github, but all of the good-first-issues seem to get grabbed up pretty quickly.

I read through the Rex paper, and immediately wondered if their extensions to the eBPF verifier (written in C) could be implemented in Rust instead.

Is this possible, and if so, what is the level of difficulty of such an attempt?

Aside from that, what ongoing projects could benefit from additional programmers / review? Is the issue tracker still the best place to find a way to contribute? What is the best way to get involved without duplicating work?

what ongoing projects could benefit from additional programmers / review? Is the issue tracker still the best place to find a way to contribute? What is the best way to get involved without duplicating work?

Essentially all patches sent to the rust-for-linux mailing list would appreciate reviews! :) Reviews are very welcome and very needed, and they are a good way to get to know the code around. So I would suggest subscribing to the mailing list and taking a look at what people submit, or looking in Lore for the latest threads: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/

As for contributing, in general, you may want to take a look at the https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing page for other ideas and suggestions.

I hope that helps.
Also testing of patches is welcome too -- the Tested-by tag is still fairly rare to be seen.

Miguel Ojeda said:

I hope that helps.

it does, I'll subscribe to the mailing list, and review some patches as they come in. thanks for the pointers.

Hello there, just joined the community. I will begin my graduation project and I was thinking in associating it with a contribution for RFS. Is there a particular subject I should focus on? I was thinking maybe network drivers or similar. What do you guys think?
I would suggest to focus on a area that you enjoy working on, or topics that either you have some experience or that you want to learn about.

I would like to focus on network drivers for example. Is there a particular one that needs work? I would like to do something that can become a contribution in the future, so I'm avoiding areas that are well developed or already have people who are already working on them

For networking, @FUJITA Tomonori , @Trevor Gross, @Valentin Obst et al. may be able to give you some ideas.

In general, if you want to upstream a driver, the easiest path is to find something that does not exist for C yet. Alternatively, you may want to port an existing one, but then the relevant maintainers will need to be on board with it, i.e. in general the kernel does not allow duplicated drivers.

But please also take a look at these links -- I have there some more context there:

https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing#submitting-new-abstractions-and-modules
https://rust-for-linux.com/rust-reference-drivers

I would also suggest searching in Lore for recent threads about the topics you are interested in: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/

I hope that helps!
Thank you for the help. I'll take a look at the links provided
Do you have any specific ideas about what you would be interested in?

Trevor Gross said:

Do you have any specific ideas about what you would be interested in?

Are you asking me, or Guilherme?
